I therefore justify the structure from the well-established Cohen/Knowles amyloid master-equation framework, which I already know: Aβ42 aggregation is dominated by monomer-dependent **secondary nucleation** producing a sigmoidal curve; the lag phase / half-time is set by when the autocatalytic (secondary) feedback ignites, whereas the growth steepness is governed by the elongation/proliferation rate. Inhibitors (e.g. Brichos, small molecules) characteristically **delay the half-time by suppressing the secondary-nucleation feedback** while leaving the plateau and often the final elongation slope largely intact.
